question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: '] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged external user open an unlimited number of concurrent gRPC subscriptions via listen() with no rate limiting or connection cap enforced, causing server-side thread/memory exhaustion and denial of service for all other subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: '] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged external user subscribe to any arbitrary topicId via listen() without any authorization check, causing unauthorized disclosure of all messages published to topics they do not own?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: '] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged external user pass a TopicMessageFilter with limit=0 (unlimited) to listen(), causing the server to stream an unbounded number of messages and exhaust heap memory or network bandwidth?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: '] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged external user pass a TopicMessageFilter with startTime=0 to listen(), causing the server to replay the entire historical message log from the beginning of time and exhaust I/O and memory resources?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: '] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged external user open thousands of simultaneous listen() subscriptions each with startTime=0 and limit=0, causing a compounded resource exhaustion attack that crashes the gRPC service for all users?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: '] [Function: filterMessage(TopicMessage, TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of an endTime check in filterMessage() to receive messages beyond the requested subscription window, causing unintended data disclosure past the intended time boundary?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: '] [Function: filterMessage(TopicMessage, TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that filterMessage() only checks topicId equality and consensusTimestamp >= startTime with no ownership or ACL validation, causing full read access to any topic's message stream?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: '] [Function: filterMessage(TopicMessage, TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged external user supply a crafted TopicMessageFilter where topicId matches a sensitive system topic, bypassing filterMessage()'s purely equality-based check with no access control, causing disclosure of sensitive consensus messages?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: '] [Function: filterMessage(TopicMessage, TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of null-safety in filterMessage() — specifically message.getTopicId().equals(filter.getTopicId()) — by triggering a NullPointerException if getTopicId() returns null, causing a denial of service for the affected subscription stream?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: '] [Function: filterMessage(TopicMessage, TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a filter where filter.getTopicId() is null, causing filterMessage() to throw a NullPointerException on the equals() call and crashing the reactive Flux pipeline for all co-subscribers on a shared hot publisher?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: '] [Function: recordMetric(TopicMessage)] Can an unprivileged external user trigger recordMetric() with a TopicMessage whose consensusTimestamp is crafted to be extremely large (near Long.MAX_VALUE), causing the division topicMessage.getConsensusTimestamp() / 1000000 to overflow or produce an astronomically large latency value that corrupts the hiero.mirror.grpc.consensus.latency timer metrics?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: '] [Function: recordMetric(TopicMessage)] Can an unprivileged external user cause recordMetric() to record arbitrarily large latency values into the MeterRegistry by subscribing to a topic with a very old startTime, polluting the consensus latency histogram and causing false alerting or metric-based DoS?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: '] [Function: recordMetric(TopicMessage)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the integer division in recordMetric() — System.currentTimeMillis() - (topicMessage.getConsensusTimestamp() / 1000000) — where truncation causes a negative latency that is silently dropped (latency > 0 check), hiding real timing anomalies and making security monitoring blind to replay attacks?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/CompositeTopicListener.java -> Scope: '] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of backpressure enforcement in the listen() Flux pipeline — which chains getTopicListener().listen(filter).filter(...).doOnNext(...) — to overwhelm a slow consumer and cause unbounded in-memory buffering leading to OutOfMemoryError?
